hero.img_alt: Full elevation of the Casa Efterpi facade, from pediment to garden
invitation.heading: Settle In.
invitation.body1: There is no rush at Casa Efterpi. Mornings begin on the stone steps with coffee and the scent of the garden; evenings end on the veranda, watching the light leave the Aegean.
invitation.body2: However long you stay, the house asks only one thing of you — that you slow down enough to meet it.
tiles.about_img_alt: The neoclassical facade of Casa Efterpi
welcome.heading: Your Home on Samos
welcome.body1: On the island of Samos, where the Aegean meets a nineteenth-century mansion, Casa Efterpi offers quiet elegance to travellers who have stopped chasing more — and started noticing everything.
welcome.body2: Set above the harbour town, the house has watched ships come and go since the 1870s. Our concept is simple: a home run by the family who has always lived in it, open just enough to share it.
quote.text: Casa Efterpi was never meant to become a hotel in the usual sense. It is our family's home, kept exactly as it was built, and shared — quietly — with those who know how to appreciate a house that has done nothing but stand still and grow more beautiful.
pillars.arch.heading: The Architecture
pillars.arch.sub: A neoclassical mansion built to outlast centuries.
pillars.arch.body: The facade greets you first — full elevation, columns, pediment, and green shutters, glowing warm in the morning light.
pillars.sea.heading: The Sea & Garden
pillars.sea.sub: The Aegean begins where the garden ends.
pillars.sea.body: Bougainvillea in the foreground, blue water beyond — breakfast on the stone steps, a book in the shade of an old olive tree.
pillars.family.heading: The Family Story
pillars.family.sub: Generations of care, in every detail.
pillars.family.body: The people who built this house are the same people who welcome you into it, generation after generation.
rooms.heading: Rooms & Suites
rooms.intro: Each room is named for the feature of the house it overlooks — the garden, the gulf, the old olive grove. Furnishings are original where possible, restored where necessary, and never replaced for the sake of fashion.
rooms.garden.name: The Garden Room
rooms.garden.desc: Ground-floor quiet, opening directly onto the bougainvillea terrace.
rooms.gulf.name: The Gulf Suite
rooms.gulf.desc: Top-floor views across the rooftops to the Aegean beyond.
rooms.olive.name: The Olive Room
rooms.olive.desc: A smaller, sunlit room beneath the old roofline, facing the olive grove.
gallery.heading: A Glimpse Inside
gallery.intro: Architecture, gardens, and the sea beyond — as the house presents them, without filters or staging.
about.heading: Generations of Care
about.body1: Casa Efterpi has belonged to our family since the house was first built — and we have never thought of ourselves as anything other than its caretakers. Every cornice, shutter, and stone step has been maintained, not modernised, by the people who grew up beneath this roof.
about.body2: We open our home to a small number of guests at a time, because that is the only way we know how to do this well. There is no front desk, no uniform, no script — only a family who happens to also welcome travellers, the way Samos has always welcomed them.
testimonials.heading: What Guests Remember
testimonials.body: We don't ask guests to leave reviews — but some write to us anyway. The house is currently being restored, and their stories will return here once we reopen our doors.
testimonials.placeholder: Guest stories are on their way. We're restoring Casa Efterpi with care, and look forward to sharing them again soon.
contact.heading: Begin Your Stay
contact.intro: Write to us directly, or use the form below. We answer every message ourselves.
contact.success: Thank you — your message has been received. We will reply personally, soon.
footer.newsletter_success: Thank you — you're on the list.
